$comment: Neutral shipped <pwd.h> (POSIX) — c81 (the sqlite shell.c CLI route; shell.c:174 `#include <pwd.h>` under !_WIN32 && !__RTP__ && !_WRS_KERNEL && !SQLITE_WASI). Surface = exactly shell.c's use: `pwent=getpwuid(getuid())` -> `pwent->pw_dir` (~ tilde expansion of the home directory, shell.c:37705). ★ struct passwd is LAYOUT-CRITICAL: libc RETURNS a pointer to ITS OWN struct and the caller reads fields at the glibc offsets — a wrong field list is a silent misread, so the layout is verified against the REAL /usr/include/pwd.h in WSL (x86_64 AND the aarch64 cross headers — IDENTICAL, both LP64): pw_name char* @0, pw_passwd char* @8, pw_uid __uid_t(u32) @16, pw_gid __gid_t(u32) @20, pw_gecos char* @24, pw_dir char* @32, pw_shell char* @40 -> sizeof 48; the layout engine derives exactly these offsets from the field sizes (two adjacent u32s pack 16..24). getpwuid takes uid_t BY VALUE (u32 — sys/types.json's uid_t) and returns the struct pointer as ptr<void> per house style (time.json/localtime precedent: standard C void*->T* coercion keeps the layout out of the signature). uid_t itself is NOT re-shipped here — sys/types.json owns it (one canonical home; shell.c includes <sys/types.h>). Gated [elf] ONLY — Darwin's struct passwd DIVERGES (10 fields: pw_change + pw_class after pw_gid, pw_expire at the tail) and no macho leg exercises shell.c, so shipping an unverified Darwin layout for a struct LIBC WRITES would be a silent-miscompile surface on an unexercised leg; a macho consumer gets fail-loud F001A/unavailable and the Darwin field list lands verified, additively, when needed. libc symbol verified in WSL: getpwuid@GLIBC_2.2.5 in libc.so.6.
